title: Plastic pollution
explanation:
Plastic pollution

Every year, millions of tonnes of plastic waste enter the oceans. Fish and birds can eat small pieces of plastic and become ill. Supermarkets now charge for plastic bags in many countries to reduce waste. People can help by using reusable bottles, recycling at home and buying products with less packaging. Small changes by many people can make a big difference.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Read the text and choose the correct answer.

questions.text: Plastic waste often ends up in the [*oceans|mountains|shops].
Animals can become [*ill|rich|fast] from eating plastic.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Read and choose the correct option.

questions.text: People should recycle at ____. | *home | sea | work only
Buy products with less ____. | *packaging | plastic only | water
Small changes can make a big ____. | *difference | problem | ocean
Birds and fish eat small ____ of plastic. | *pieces | bags | bottles

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Read the text and choose the correct answer.

questions.text: Charging for bags is meant to [*reduce|increase|ignore] waste.
